preamble: The shift from [d] to [t] via initial devoicing is a widespread phenomenon that drives Tonogenesis by creating distinct pitch registers. syallables originally beginning with [d] are assigned to the lower register ('Yáng') while those starting with [t] are assigned to the higher register ('Yīn').
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Register Split, Tonogenesis, Voice Loss

examples.note: Led to the split of the historic tones into Low and High registers based on initial voicing.
Part of the systemic devoicing of initial obstruents which doubled the tonal categories.

examples.to: t- (aspirated or unaspirated based on tone)
examples.note: The low register of modern Cantonese tones reflects original voicing in the initial.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
